name: Australian History
description: From early European contact through colonization, federation, and modern Australia

questions.q: In what year did Captain Arthur Phillip's First Fleet arrive at Sydney Cove?

source.notes: Arrived January 26, 1788 — now observed as Australia Day.

questions.q: What is the name of the sacred red sandstone rock in Australia's Northern Territory?

source.notes: Sacred to the Aṉangu people; renamed officially to Uluru/Ayers Rock in 1993.

questions.q: Which Queensland reef, the world's largest coral reef system, is visible from space?

source.notes: More than 2,900 individual reefs across 344,000 km².

questions.q: Which Summer Olympic Games did Sydney host?

source.notes: Cathy Freeman lit the cauldron and later won gold in the 400m.

questions.q: What is the name of the Sydney performing arts venue famous for its sail-shaped shells?

source.notes: Designed by Danish architect Jørn Utzon; opened 1973.

questions.q: Which armored bushranger was hanged in Melbourne in 1880?

source.notes: Last stand at Glenrowan, June 1880; hanged November 11 at Old Melbourne Gaol.

questions.q: Which British explorer charted Australia's east coast in 1770 on HMS Endeavour?

source.notes: Landed at Botany Bay April 29, 1770.

questions.q: In what year did the Australian colonies federate into the Commonwealth of Australia?

source.notes: Proclaimed January 1, 1901.

questions.q: Who served as Australia's first Prime Minister?

source.notes: Served 1901-1903, from NSW.

questions.q: The 1854 Eureka Stockade rebellion by gold miners took place near which Victorian town?

source.notes: Miners protested mining licenses; led to manhood suffrage reforms.

questions.q: Which Dutch navigator made the first confirmed European sighting of the Australian mainland in 1606?

source.notes: Aboard the Duyfken; charted part of the Cape York Peninsula.

questions.q: Which iconic Sydney bridge, nicknamed 'The Coathanger,' opened in 1932?

source.notes: Chief engineer John Bradfield; opened March 19, 1932.

questions.q: The Sydney Opera House officially opened in what year?

source.notes: Opened October 20, 1973 by Queen Elizabeth II.

questions.q: What was Tasmania known as from 1642 until the mid-19th century?

source.notes: Named by Abel Tasman for the Governor-General of the Dutch East Indies; renamed Tasmania in 1856.

questions.q: Which Australian Prime Minister was controversially dismissed by the Governor-General in 1975?

source.notes: Dismissed November 11, 1975 by Governor-General Sir John Kerr.

questions.q: Which landmark 1986 act removed the UK Parliament's ability to legislate for Australia?

source.notes: Passed in both UK and Australian parliaments; completed legislative independence.

questions.q: Approximately how many British convicts were transported to Australia during the period of penal transportation?

source.notes: Transportation ran 1788-1868; final convicts arrived in WA.

questions.q: In what year did the legendary Australian racehorse Phar Lap win the Melbourne Cup?

source.notes: Died suddenly in California in 1932 under suspicious circumstances.

questions.q: Archaeological evidence at the Madjedbebe rock shelter suggests Aboriginal people have lived in Australia for approximately how many years?

source.notes: 2017 study pushed human occupation back from ~50k to ~65k years.

questions.q: Who was elected in 1971 as Australia's first Indigenous member of Parliament?

source.notes: Senator for Queensland, Liberal Party.

questions.q: Canonized in 2010, who became Australia's first Roman Catholic saint?

source.notes: Co-founded the Sisters of St Joseph of the Sacred Heart; canonized by Pope Benedict XVI.

questions.q: The 1932-33 England cricket tour of Australia became infamous under which name, featuring short-pitched bowling aimed at batsmen's bodies?

source.notes: Tactic devised by captain Douglas Jardine to neutralize Don Bradman.
